evals.prompt: I'm on Scandit SDK v6 and need to migrate my BarcodeCapture Capacitor integration to v7. Here's my scanner file: ScannerApp_v6.js
evals.expected_output: The skill reads migration.md, applies the 6→7 changes to ScannerApp_v6.js (the v6→v7 path is largely additive — the legacy factories still work), and summarizes any changes made.

assertions.text: BarcodeCapture.forContext( is still acceptable in the output, or was upgraded to new BarcodeCapture(settings) plus context.setMode( (semantic)
BarcodeCaptureOverlay.withBarcodeCaptureForView( is still acceptable in the output, or was upgraded to new BarcodeCaptureOverlay( plus view.addOverlay( (semantic)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is still acceptable in the output (v7 keeps it valid)
A summary.md file is present describing changes (or stating that no source changes were strictly required)
Summary mentions the package.json dependency versions need to be bumped to ^7.x and npx cap sync must be run

evals.prompt: We're upgrading our Capacitor app from Scandit SDK 7 to 8. Here's our BarcodeCapture code: ScannerApp_v7.js. Please apply any necessary changes.
evals.expected_output: The skill applies the v7→v8 renames (DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey → DataCaptureContext.initialize, BarcodeCapture.forContext → new BarcodeCapture + context.setMode, BarcodeCaptureOverlay.withBarcodeCaptureForView → new BarcodeCaptureOverlay + view.addOverlay) and summarizes the changes.

assertions.text: DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present in the output file
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present in the output file
new BarcodeCapture( is present in the output file
BarcodeCapture.forContext( is NOT present in the output file
context.setMode( is called on the BarcodeCapture instance
new BarcodeCaptureOverlay( is present in the output file
BarcodeCaptureOverlay.withBarcodeCaptureForView( is NOT present in the output file
view.addOverlay( is called
A summary.md is present listing the renames
Summary mentions npx cap sync must be run after the plugin version bump
The output file is functionally equivalent to the input otherwise — no unnecessary changes

evals.prompt: We need to upgrade our BarcodeCapture integration from SDK 6 all the way to v8. Here's our current code: ScannerApp_v6.js
evals.expected_output: The skill applies both migrations in order (6→7 then 7→8) and summarizes all changes organized by migration step.

assertions.text: DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (7→8 rename applied)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
new BarcodeCapture( is present (7→8 rename applied)
BarcodeCapture.forContext( is NOT present
context.setMode( is called on the BarcodeCapture instance
new BarcodeCaptureOverlay( is present (7→8 rename applied)
BarcodeCaptureOverlay.withBarcodeCaptureForView( is NOT present
view.addOverlay( is called
Summary covers both migration steps (references both 6→7 and 7→8)

evals.prompt: I want to upgrade my BarcodeCapture to the latest Scandit SDK version. Here's my code and package.json.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads package.json to detect v6.28.x, determines the target is v8, applies both migrations without asking the user for their version.

assertions.text: Summary mentions the detected version (6.28.x or v6)
Summary mentions the package.json dependency versions need to be bumped to ^8.0.0
Summary mentions npx cap sync must be run after the bump
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (7→8 rename applied)
new BarcodeCapture( is present (7→8 rename applied)
context.setMode( is called on the BarcodeCapture instance
new BarcodeCaptureOverlay( is present (7→8 rename applied)
view.addOverlay( is called
Did not ask the user for their version — proceeded autonomously

evals.prompt: Upgrade my BarcodeCapture from v7 to v8 — here's the package.json and the source.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads package.json to detect v7.6.0, determines the target is v8, and applies only the 7→8 migration.

assertions.text: Summary mentions the detected version (7.6.x or v7)
Summary mentions the package.json dependency versions need to be bumped to ^8.0.0
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
new BarcodeCapture( is present
BarcodeCapture.forContext( is NOT present
new BarcodeCaptureOverlay( is present
BarcodeCaptureOverlay.withBarcodeCaptureForView( is NOT present
Summary mentions npx cap sync must be run after the bump
